theory.intro: A unit fraction has a numerator of $1$. Finding a unit fraction of a quantity means sharing the quantity into that many equal parts.
facts: $\tfrac{1}{n}$ of a quantity is the quantity **divided by $n$**.
$\tfrac{1}{2}$ means split into $2$ equal parts, $\tfrac{1}{4}$ into $4$, and so on.
A bar split into $n$ equal segments shows each segment as $\tfrac{1}{n}$ of the whole.
steps: Read the denominator to see how many equal parts to make.
Divide the quantity by that number.
The result is one of the equal parts.
